question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `RouteMatcher` type assertion (`next.(middleware.RouteMatcher)`) to send requests to routes that do not implement `RouteMatcher`, causing a runtime panic in `MetricsMiddleware` that crashes the server and halts block ingestion for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with deeply nested URL paths that do not match any registered route, causing `RouteMatcher` to perform expensive regex matching on every request and delaying block-processing handlers by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared `prometheus.DefaultRegisterer` used in `init()` to register conflicting metrics from a co-located process, causing `MustRegister` to panic and crash the Rosetta service, freezing block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `ws` label value of `true` (via WebSocket upgrade headers) to create a separate label dimension in `requestDurationHistogram`, causing the histogram to track WebSocket and HTTP durations separately and doubling memory usage, which delays block-processing goroutines by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET request to `/metrics` with a very large number of query parameters to cause `promhttp.Handler()` to parse and discard them in a loop, consuming CPU cycles that delay block-processing goroutines by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause `requestDurationHistogram` to record durations in the 2.5-5 second range consistently, causing monitoring systems to interpret the node as near-overloaded and triggering automated back-pressure that delays block submission for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of per-IP rate limiting in `MetricsMiddleware` to send a sustained flood of requests that keeps `requestInflightGauge` permanently above a threshold, causing the Rosetta node to appear overloaded to Coinbase Rosetta SDK clients and halting block queries for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `route` label value containing special Prometheus label characters (e.g., `{`, `}`, `=`) to cause label parsing errors in `requestBytesHistogram`, corrupting the metric registry and delaying block-processing instrumentation for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a body size exactly at each `sizeBuckets` boundary (512, 1024, 10240, 25600, 51200 bytes) in rapid succession to maximize histogram bucket-boundary comparisons in `requestBytesHistogram`, causing CPU saturation that delays block-processing handlers by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `middleware.Instrument` wrapper to send requests that trigger slow response writes, causing `responseBytesHistogram` to hold a write lock for extended periods and blocking concurrent block-serving requests for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated `/metrics` endpoint to scrape `hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_duration` histogram data and identify the exact block-processing window, then time a request flood to coincide with block finalization, delaying that block by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `method` label value of an extremely long custom HTTP method string to cause `requestInflightGauge` label storage to grow unboundedly, exhausting memory and freezing block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of requests that each hold the connection open for exactly 5 seconds (the max `requestDurationHistogram` bucket), causing the `+Inf` bucket to accumulate rapidly and the histogram sum to grow unboundedly, corrupting block-time statistics and masking the 500% delay threshold?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `middleware.Instrument{RouteMatcher: next.(middleware.RouteMatcher)}` field to send requests to a route that returns a nil `RouteMatcher`, causing a nil pointer dereference in the instrumentation layer that crashes the server and halts block ingestion for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `Content-Type` header that causes the `middleware.Instrument` wrapper to misidentify the request as a WebSocket connection, setting `ws=true` in `requestDurationHistogram` labels and creating a cardinality explosion that delays block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a body that is exactly 1 byte larger than each `sizeBuckets` boundary to cause `requestBytesHistogram` to always record in the `+Inf` bucket, corrupting size distribution statistics and masking block-delay anomalies that would otherwise trigger the 500% threshold alert?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent GET requests to `/metrics` while simultaneously flooding block-processing endpoints, causing `promhttp.Handler()` to compete with block handlers for the same Prometheus registry lock, serializing all operations and delaying block finalization by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `requestInflightGauge` decrement logic in `middleware.Instrument` to send requests that abort mid-flight (via TCP RST), causing the gauge to never decrement and permanently showing elevated inflight counts that trigger false overload conditions, freezing block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `route` label value that is a valid Prometheus label but contains Unicode characters, causing `requestDurationHistogram` to allocate multi-byte label strings for every request and exhausting memory, delaying block-processing goroutines by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `status_code` label value of `0` (by aborting the connection before a response is sent) to cause `requestDurationHistogram` to record a zero-status label combination, creating a new label set that consumes additional memory and delays block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a body size of exactly 0 bytes to cause `requestBytesHistogram` to record in the lowest bucket (512), and then send requests with a body of 51200 bytes to cause recording in the `+Inf` bucket, alternating rapidly to maximize lock contention and delay block processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `middleware.Instrument` wrapper's lack of request body size validation to send requests with a `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` body that never terminates, holding the `requestInflightGauge` elevated indefinitely and starving block-processing goroutines for 500%+ of average block time?
